issues: The draft headline 'Kings Camp Notes' is not confirmed by the OCR — no clear headline is visible in the extracted text, just mid-article paragraphs.

The draft short description is too generic and buries the Sockeyes-relevant content (Bill Hardy joining Red Deer Kings). Since this is a Sockeyes archive, the description should foreground that connection.

The long description mentions 'Ferris State College in Chicago' — Ferris State is in Big Rapids, Michigan, not Chicago. The OCR says 'Chicago' but this is likely an OCR/print error; however we should reflect the OCR faithfully.
The draft omits mention of the overall camp context (Kings = Red Deer Kings of the AJHL) which helps orient the reader.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: A Red Deer Kings 1987 training-camp notes column reports that former Richmond Sockeyes winger Bill Hardy has joined the Kings' roster alongside other newcomers and returning veterans.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: A Red Deer Advocate camp-notes column from September 4, 1987 covers the Red Deer Kings (AJHL) training camp, listing returning veterans and newcomers. Among the newcomers highlighted is Bill Hardy, a winger who played for the Richmond Sockeyes, making this piece directly relevant to the 1987 Sockeyes alumni trail. Other notable mentions include goaltender Glenn Raeburn (formerly of Ferris State College) and defenseman Kevin Forsen (Hobbema Hawks), while veteran defensemen Jason Edgar, Hall, Jenzen, Straub, Orion Donison, and Doug Gizoski are returning.
review.notes: The OCR begins mid-article with no visible headline or byline captured — editor should check the full scan image for a headline above these paragraphs. The Sockeyes connection is Bill Hardy (#16, assistant captain) moving to the Red Deer Kings after the 1987 championship season. "Ferris State College in Chicago" in the OCR is almost certainly a print/OCR error (Ferris State is in Big Rapids, MI); the corrected description omits the city to avoid propagating the error.
